metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_652
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Dorfin binds to PSD-95 (Figure 1)
Dorfin null mutant mice show normal brain and dendrite morphology (Figure 2)
Dorfin null mutant mice show increased mEPSC amplitude and enhanced LTP in the hippocampal DG region. mEPSC frequency and paired-pulse ratio is not affected. Neither is mIPSC amplitude/frequency (Figure 4).

23/5/2017 Pim
- Dorfin is a known E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase
- Fig.7 25 proteins were shown to be under-phosphorylated in hippcampal lysates.
- So, dorfin is a known E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase that is active in hippocampus, interacts with PSD-95, and affects postsynaptic, but not presynaptic function of Glutamatergic synapses.. Experimental description: "Dorfin-/- mice were generated from mouse sperm with the gene trap cassette inserted into the intron between exon 2 and exon 3 of the Dorfin gene, obtained from TIGM (OST244733). Mice in the genetic background of 129/SvEv were backcrossed with C57BL6 mice for more than five generations."
